audience: SMEs, construction companies, property professionals, event organisers and marketing teams considering professional drone photography, conventional drone video or FPV
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Een bedrijf helpen bepalen welk type dronebeeld past, hoe een productie wordt voorbereid en welke operationele, wettelijke en privacyvoorwaarden vooraf moeten worden gecontroleerd

searchIntent.question: Which type of professional drone footage suits our business, and how should we plan a safe, lawful and useful production?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking decision-makers in Belgium and nearby markets, with current European and Belgian drone requirements retained

directAnswer: Drone footage adds value when height, movement or spatial context helps viewers understand a site, property, project or event. Choose photography, conventional drone video or FPV from the communication goal, then assess the route, current geozones, people, privacy, weather and required outputs before confirming the flight.
title: Drone footage for businesses: a complete guide to photography, video and FPV
metaDescription: Compare drone photography, conventional video and FPV, then plan professional business drone footage around current Belgian rules, privacy and delivery needs.
h1: Drone footage for businesses: a complete guide to photography, video and FPV
factsToPreserve: The guide does not grant permission for a specific flight and is not legal advice
European drone categories are based on operational risk rather than commercial purpose
Open category conditions include a general 120 metre height limit, VLOS and no flight over an assembly of people
Current Belgian geozones and overlapping access conditions must be checked before every flight
FPV in the Open category requires VLOS and a visual observer during genuine FPV flight
Aviation permission does not itself authorise publication of all recorded material
